TSLA: Tata adds NXP to wafer fab discussions, says report .... India's Tata Electronics is reportedly in discussions with NXP to become a foundry supplier for the Dutch chip giant, as Tata constructs a wafer fab in Gujurat set to begin production in 2026. This potential deal follows a similar collaboration with Analog Devices Inc. and aligns with a broader trend of fab-lite companies seeking local manufacturing, positioning Tata to benefit from a shift in equipment manufacturing away from China. Electric vehicle maker Tesla Inc. is also mentioned as a potential customer for Tata chips.

Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 04/27/2025. This article lists the top 10 performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending April 27, 2025, with returns ranging from 18.05% to 21.95%. Key drivers for these performances include caution around growth, tariffs, trade uncertainty, big tech earnings, Tesla's sales drop, increased market volatility, U.S. export restrictions on AI chips, and safe-haven demand for gold. The article details specific ETFs like SSG, TSLQ, UVIX, and NVDS, explaining the factors contributing to their strong weekly gains.

Forget Tesla: This 1 Auto Stock Is an Outperformer in 2025. While Tesla faces challenges in 2025 due to slowing deliveries, political distractions, and competition, Ferrari N.V. (RACE) is highlighted as an outperformer. Ferrari's strong demand from affluent clientele, robust guidance, and upcoming EV launch position it for continued success despite economic headwinds and tariffs. Analysts widely recommend Ferrari as a "Moderate Buy" with significant upside potential.

Tesla hiring over 1,000 workers to ramp up Semi truck production, Business Insider reports. Tesla plans to hire over 1,000 workers in Nevada to increase the mass production of its Semi electric trucks, as reported by Business Insider. The company recently announced that initial production of the Semi trucks will begin at the Gigafactory in Sparks, Nevada, by the end of 2025, aiming for an annual capacity of 50,000 units. This move signifies a significant ramp-up in Tesla's long-delayed truck-making business, which initially aimed for 2019 production.
TGT: Target CEO rakes in $20.4M as boycott deepens and Black leaders demand accountability. Target CEO Brian Cornell received a $20.4 million pay package last year, a 6.3% increase, amidst a deepening boycott by Black leaders and activists. The boycott stems from Target's rollback of its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) commitments, including a highly publicized $2 billion pledge to support Black-owned businesses. Activists are demanding accountability as Target's stock price drops and its responses to community concerns are perceived as insufficient.

These spring picks are stylist-approved! An 83% Vera Bradley discount, 'sailor' jeans, more. Style expert Jasmine Snow shared her top affordable spring fashion picks from Target, highlighting versatile clothing items and accessories. The recommendations include a discounted Vera Bradley bag, "sailor" jeans, raffia mules, and elevated men's pieces, all aimed at refreshing wardrobes for the season without breaking the bank. The article details where to purchase these items and their key features.
SBUX: High-performing grocery-anchored retail center in Los Angeles secures $21.5M refinancing. JLL Capital Markets has successfully arranged $21.5 million in refinancing for the Juanita Tate Marketplace, a 77,096-square-foot grocery-anchored retail center in Los Angeles. The financing was secured through institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management on behalf of the borrower, Optimus Properties, LLC. The marketplace is 100% leased, anchored by Northgate Market, and includes tenants like CVS, Panda Express, Starbucks, and Chase Bank, benefiting from high visibility and a densely populated surrounding area.

This low-profile CEO is the highest-paid in America with a $101 million paycheck that beat out Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple chiefs. Jim Anderson, the CEO of Coherent, has emerged as America's highest-paid CEO with a 2024 compensation package totaling over $101 million, largely due to stock awards. This figure surpassed the earnings of well-known executives like those from Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple. Anderson's low-profile company, Coherent, specializes in network and laser equipment, and his compensation highlights a rising trend in CEO pay driven by significant stock payouts.

Why Starbucks Stock Fell 18% in April. Starbucks (SBUX) stock dropped 18% in April due to President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs announcement and an underwhelming fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Despite management's claims of a recovery strategy, the company missed revenue and earnings estimates, with comparable sales and adjusted operating margin declining. CEO Brian Niccol aims to turn the business around amidst macro headwinds.

April earnings update: Robinhood, Sleep Number, Starbucks, Ally Financial and State Street. This article compiles insights from CFOs during their April earnings calls, highlighting strategic moves, financial performance, and future outlooks for several companies. Key discussions include Robinhood's strong retail investor engagement, Sleep Number's cost reduction plan, Starbucks' turnaround strategy, and Ally Financial's efforts to boost net interest margin. Other companies like Independent Bank, State Street, First Industrial Realty Trust, Fastenal, and Guaranty Bancshares also shared updates on competitive markets, expense flexibility, debt restructuring, and loan loss reserves.

Starbucks outlook shifts to negative, Moody’s affirms ratings. Moody's Ratings has changed Starbucks' outlook from stable to negative due to weakening profitability and credit metrics, stemming from sales deleveraging and increased labor investments as part of its "Back to Starbucks" reinvention plan. Despite the negative outlook, Moody's affirmed all of Starbucks' ratings, including Baa1 senior unsecured notes and Prime-2 commercial paper program, citing the company's strong brand, market position, and liquidity. However, challenges like heightened competition, cost pressures, and consumer spending uncertainty could lead to a downgrade if comparable sales weaken or financial policies become more aggressive.

Circular Action Alliance adds to founding members. The Circular Action Alliance (CAA), a nonprofit producer responsibility organization, has announced The Campbell’s Co., Ikea Supply AG, and Starbucks Corp. as new founding members. These companies will also gain representation on CAA's board, strengthening its governance as it implements extended producer responsibility (EPR) laws for packaging in various U.S. states. CAA operates as a PRO in California, Colorado, Oregon, and Minnesota, playing a critical role in representing producer interests in EPR programs.

Starbucks Profit Drops, but Leaders Say Turnaround Is Working. Starbucks reported a 50 percent drop in quarterly profit, but its CEO, Brian Niccol, asserted that the company's turnaround strategy is showing progress. Despite challenges like higher coffee prices and tariffs, global same-store sales improved, and the company is implementing initiatives like hiring more baristas and testing order-sequencing programs to enhance customer experience. The profit decline was partly attributed to investments in additional workers for the turnaround and restructuring costs.
DIS: Fubo Reports Subscriber Loss. FuboTV announced its first-quarter results, reporting a global streaming business that exceeded subscriber guidance and improved profitability metrics despite a loss of subscribers. North American paid subscribers were down 2.7% year-over-year, while Rest of World subscribers saw a 10.9% decrease. The company also highlighted its agreement with Disney to combine Fubo with Hulu + Live TV, a deal that included a $220 million payment to end antitrust litigation and a commitment for a $145 million term loan from Disney in 2026.

Brand Creates Exclusive Collectible Cans in celebration of Disney’s “Lilo & Stitch” Live-Action Movie. The SPAM® brand has announced a collaboration with Disney's upcoming live-action "Lilo & Stitch" movie, releasing exclusive Hawaiian-inspired limited-edition collectible cans featuring artwork from the film. This partnership also includes a new TV commercial campaign starting May 5, celebrating the cultural connection both the SPAM® brand and Stitch share with Hawaiian traditions and family. The collectible cans are available nationwide, with the movie set to premiere in theaters on May 23, 2025.

The Walt Disney Company To Participate In The Moffettnathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The Walt Disney Company announced that Josh D’Amaro, Chairman, Disney Experiences, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The session is scheduled for Wednesday, May 14, 2025, at approximately 9:40 a.m. ET/ 6:40 a.m. PT. A live stream will be available on the company's investor relations website, where an archived recording will also be accessible.
